content.full_text:
October 7, 1991

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

RE: REQUEST FOR INFORMAL ASSISTANCE

Dear Scott:

This firm represents a recent appointee by Governor Wilson to the California Arts Council. On behalf of our client, we would like to request informal assistance pursuant to FPPC Regulation 18329(c) with respect to the new honoraria prohibition and gift limit provisions of the Political Reform Act (Government Code sections 89503 and 89505).

Government Code section 89503(a) provides that "[n]o member of a state board or commission...shall accept any honorarium from any source, if the member...would be required to report the receipt of income or gifts from that source on his or her statement of economic interest." Similarly, Government Code section 89505(a) provides that "[n]o member of a state board or commission...shall accept gifts with a total value of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) in a calendar year from any single source, if the member...would be required to report the receipt of income or gifts from that source on his or her statement of economic interest."

Members of the California Arts Council are only required to disclose interests in nonprofit arts organizations. (See letter from Jeanette Turvill dated October 4, 1991.)

Accordingly, we would like to confirm that, in the case of the California Arts Council, these statutory provisions mean that the honoraria prohibition and the gift limit apply only to sources of honoraria or gifts which are nonprofit arts organizations.

Scott Hallabrin  
October 7, 1991  
Page 2

Please feel free to contact me if you need additional information or would like to discuss further.

Thank you for your consideration of this request.

Sincerely,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

[Signature]
DIANE M. FISHBURN
DMF/ccz
